More TED talks on AI
Listen to the episode

Transcript-summary pages are useful only when the editorial framing survives the transcript scaffolding that surrounds it. Readers need the argument, not just the interface around the spoken version.

In this case, the argument is that what matters after launch is who reviews exceptions and how teams recover when models drift, not just how impressive the system looked on stage.

Once you strip away the navigation and talk prompts, the piece reads like an operating memo about accountability, supervision, and recovery under load.
